title: Controlled S1 recall expansion for object-oriented and import edges
goal: Evaluate inheritance, mixin, super, receiver-alias and selected import patterns as separately reason-coded candidates, promoting only classes that preserve the registered S1 precision threshold.

acceptance.assertion: Inheritance, mixins, super calls, receiver aliases, star imports, module __getattr__ and transitive imports are measured as separate reason classes rather than one broad heuristic.
A reason class becomes S1 only when the fixed goldset meets at least 0.97 precision with no registered case regression; otherwise candidates remain explicit S0.
Dynamic attribute dispatch, monkey patching, ambiguous multiple inheritance and unresolved star exports remain fail-closed and are never promoted by name similarity alone.
The evaluation reports per-reason precision, recall, abstention and false-positive examples and preserves the no-transitive-import-resolution non-claim unless a bounded resolver is separately proven.

metadata.source_request: Post-merge reviews for Lenskit PR #1018 were live-verified; confirmed optimization work is registered separately from the corrective follow-up.

bureau_always_open_scope.reason: Keep Bureau intake and unrelated registry writes available while Lenskit implementation runs in an isolated worktree.
